content.problemStatement:
There are NNN positions along a line, each of which contains either a building (with positive height) or a volcano.

You are given this information as an array AAA of length NNN, where Ai=0A_i = 0Ai‚Äã=0 represents a volcano at position iii, and Ai>0A_i \gt 0Ai‚Äã>0 represents a building with height AiA_iAi‚Äã at position iii.

Initially, the lava level is 000. Every second, all the volcanoes erupt simultaneously, increasing each of their lava levels by exactly PPP.

Lava behaves as follows:


Suppose the current lava level is XXX. Then, lava will flow left and and right from each volcano till it reaches a building whose height is **strictly greater** than XXX, at which point it will stop flowing.
For each iii, determine the minimum time at which building iii is completely submerged under lava.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line contains two space-separated integers NNN and PPP ‚Äî the number of buildings and the rate at which lava level increases, respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã, describing information about the buildings and volcanoes as detailed in the statement.




Output Format

For each test case, output NNN space-separated integers, say t1,t2,‚Ä¶,tNt_1, t_2, \ldots, t_Nt1‚Äã,t2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,tN‚Äã.

If Ai=0A_i = 0Ai‚Äã=0, then tit_iti‚Äã should also be 000.
If Ai>0A_i \gt 0Ai‚Äã>0, tit_iti‚Äã should be the minimum time at which building iii is completely covered by lava.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all test cases will not exceed 3√ó1053 \times 10^53√ó105.
There exists at least one index iii such that Ai=0A_i = 0Ai‚Äã=0.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The lava behaves as follows:

In the first second, the lava level increases by P=2P = 2P=2 and becomes 222. No buildings are submerged.
In the next second, the lava level increases by P=2P = 2P=2 and becomes 444. The first building is now submerged since the lava from the volcano at index 222 will flow over it.
In the third second, the lava level increases by 222 again, and becomes 666. The lava from the volcano at 222 moves right till it reaches index 555; submerging buildings 333 and 444.
In the fourth second, the lava level becomes 888, and the final remaining building is submerged.

So, we obtain t1=2t_1 = 2t1‚Äã=2, t3=t4=3t_3 = t_4 = 3t3‚Äã=t4‚Äã=3, and t5=4t_5 = 4t5‚Äã=4.
